_meta.rule_cite: CLAUDE.md Build rules: 'Allow-list not deny-list for public-API response shaping' + 'Public-shape snapshot with drift-check command is MANDATORY for every NEW or SHAPE-MODIFIED public API endpoint'

TIER_LIMIT_REACHED.fires_when: free-tier caller invokes a tool after currentUsage >= monthlyLimit (license.ts:checkQuota returns allowed:false)
TIER_LIMIT_REACHED.interaction_with_tier_warning: tier_warning is NEVER set in this error path; the error envelope itself carries the upgrade context

CHAT_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED.fires_when: chat-tool monthly quota exhausted (separate from MCP tool quota; lives at index.ts:466-473)
CHAT_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED.interaction_with_tier_warning: chat-tool quota is internal to the chat tool flow; tier_warning on _algovault NOT fired by chat path per ACTIVATION-PAYWALL-W1 scope (chat-quota lane explicitly out of scope)

cache_contract.tier_warning_freshness: computed per-request from in-process callTrackers (license.ts); no caching layer between trackCall() and tier_warning emission
cache_contract.stale_signal_handling: n/a — quota counter is monotonically incremented in-process; bot restart resets counters (handled at next month-boundary)

consumers.type: MCP client (Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor / Cline)
consumers.expected_behavior: Read `_algovault.tier_warning.suggested_upgrade_url` and surface as a CTA when present. Display `_algovault.tier_warning.level` ('soft' / 'hard') to drive UX prominence (e.g., subtle bottom-bar at 'soft', banner at 'hard'). When `tier_warning` is OMITTED, do nothing — absence of field is normal for paid tiers / bot-internal / below-threshold callers.

Pattern-match on `_algovault.tier_warning.level === 'hard'` to inform the end-user before making more tool calls that quota is near. Do NOT block tool invocation on `tier_warning` — only the explicit `TIER_LIMIT_REACHED` error envelope blocks.

Bot internal calls (`X-AlgoVault-Internal-Key` header) bypass `tier_warning` entirely — the field will never appear in bot responses. Bot has its own per-user quota tracker in SQLite (`subscribers.alerts_24h_*` columns) for end-user quota enforcement.

drift_check_command: curl -fsS https://api.algovault.com/mcp -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer <FREE_TIER_TEST_KEY>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' --data '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"get_trade_call","arguments":{"coin":"BTC","timeframe":"15m"}}}' | jq -r '.result.content[0].text' | jq -r '._algovault | keys | sort | join(",")' | { read keys; ALLOWED='compatible_with,exchange,session_id,tier_warning,tool,venue_status,version'; if [ "$keys" = "$ALLOWED" ] || [ "$keys" = 'compatible_with,exchange,session_id,tool,venue_status,version' ]; then echo 'DRIFT_CHECK_OK'; else echo "DRIFT_CHECK_FAIL: got '$keys'; expected '$ALLOWED' (with tier_warning) or '$ALLOWED' minus tier_warning (when below soft threshold or paid tier)"; exit 1; fi; }
drift_check_notes: tier_warning is OPTIONAL — its presence depends on free-tier+75%+threshold conditions. The drift-check accepts BOTH the with-tier_warning and without-tier_warning shapes.
upgrade_hint, exchange, venue_status, session_id are also OPTIONAL — drift-check tolerates their omission per existing semantics.
Run from operator machine with a FREE_TIER_TEST_KEY at 80+ calls/month to trigger the with-tier_warning shape; from a paid-tier key to trigger the without shape.

tier_warning_shape.suggested_upgrade_url: string (Stripe Checkout via /signup?plan=starter with utm_source+utm_campaign for attribution)

test_coverage: tests/unit/algovault-meta-tier-warning.test.ts (17 cases: thresholds, paid tiers, bot-internal, immutability, defensive NaN/zero/negative)
